title: chat_using() OnceLock adds complexity with no benefit
description: methods.rs: chat_using() uses OnceLock<Vec<String>> to cache two static strings, but build_request() calls .to_vec() on the result every time anyway. The OnceLock saves one Vec allocation per process lifetime while adding indirection and a non-obvious two-call pattern. Suggested rewrite: remove chat_using(), add const CHAT_USING: [&str; 2] = ["urn:ietf:params:jmap:core", "urn:ietf:params:jmap:chat"]; and have build_request() do CHAT_USING.iter().map(|&s| s.to_string()).collect().
close_reason: Opinion: OnceLock vs const array is a style preference with identical correctness
